title: Buyer-Seller Messaging
description: Buyers and sellers exchange messages in typed threads (inquiry, direct, offer, custom request) with safety filtering, unread tracking, and archiving.

flows.title: Open Messaging
flows.trigger: User opens inbox or clicks Message on a listing

steps.title: Open Messaging
steps.notes: Entry point for all messaging flows. Accessible from the nav inbox badge or from a listing detail page CTA. Full inbox UI is Phase 3 — API layer is built. See planned inbox-page step in view-inbox flow.

flows.title: View Thread Inbox
flows.trigger: User opens the inbox page or navigates to /dashboard/messages

steps.title: Fetch Thread List
steps.tooltip: Returns all threads where user is a participant, joined with participant member details and my_unread_count. Optional ?type= filter accepts: inquiry, direct, offer, custom_request.

steps.title: Fetch Unread Count
steps.tooltip: Sum of unread_count across all active participant rows for this user. Drives the inbox badge in the navigation header. Query key: ['messaging', 'unread'].

steps.title: Show Inbox
steps.notes: Inbox page UI is Phase 3. Renders thread list with participant avatars, last message preview, unread badge per thread, and type filter tabs (All / Inquiries / Offers / Direct). ThreadWithParticipants already carries my_unread_count and last_message_preview from the API.

steps.title: Show Empty Inbox
steps.notes: Empty state when user has no threads. Should show encouraging CTA to browse listings. Distinct from filtered-empty state (all threads exist but the active filter shows none).

flows.title: Start a New Thread
flows.trigger: Buyer clicks Message on a listing detail page, or member initiates direct message

steps.title: Create Thread
steps.tooltip: Body: { type, participantIds, roles, listingId?, shopId? }. Returns 201 for new thread, 409 if a thread with the same participants and type already exists (client should redirect to the existing thread). type must be one of: inquiry, direct, offer, custom_request. listingId required for inquiry; shopId required for custom_request.

errorCases.condition: roles length does not match participantIds

errorCases.result: Returns 409 — client redirects to existing thread

steps.title: Open New Thread
steps.notes: On 201: navigate to the new thread view. On 409: redirect to the existing thread (the response body contains the existing ThreadWithParticipants). The client service createThread() returns the thread in both cases — callers should differentiate via HTTP status if needed.

flows.title: Open a Thread
flows.trigger: User clicks a thread row in the inbox, or navigates directly to a thread

steps.title: Fetch Thread Details
steps.tooltip: Returns ThreadWithParticipants: thread row joined with participant member details (name, avatar, slug) and my_unread_count. Returns 404 if the thread does not exist or user is not a participant (participant check is in getThreadByIdServer).

errorCases.condition: Thread not found or user not a participant

steps.title: Fetch Messages
steps.tooltip: Cursor-based pagination. Query params: ?cursor={messageId}&limit={1-100, default 50}. Returns { messages: MessageWithSender[], nextCursor: string | null }. Messages ordered newest-first. Non-participant request returns 403.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

steps.title: Mark Thread Read
steps.tooltip: Resets unread_count to 0 on the user's message_thread_participants row. Called automatically on thread open. Drives the unread badge update in the nav — client should invalidate ['messaging', 'unread'] after success.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

steps.title: Render Thread View
steps.notes: MessageThread component scaffold exists at src/features/messaging/components/message-thread/index.tsx. Full UI is Phase 3. Should render participant header, paginated message list (infinite scroll via useMessages hook), and the compose input.

flows.title: Send a Message
flows.trigger: User types and submits a message in an open thread

steps.title: Compose Message
steps.notes: Message compose input in the thread view. Phase 3 UI. Should show character count and send button. Optimistic insert via useSendMessage hook — message appears immediately before server confirm.

steps.title: Send Message
steps.tooltip: Body: { content, type? }. type defaults to 'text'. Runs participant check, block check, and safety filter (block → 422, redact → stored filtered, nudge → nudge system message appended, pass → as-is). Returns 201 MessageWithSender.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

errorCases.result: Returns 403 — cannot send to this user

steps.title: Apply Safety Filter
steps.tooltip: Processing order: block → redact → nudge → pass. Block: explicit/profane content → 422 rejected. Redact: PII (phone, email, credit card via Luhn) → replaced with [removed], original stored in original_content with is_filtered=true. Nudge: off-platform dealing or price negotiation → message passes but a nudge system message is inserted after it. Pass: no match → stored as-is.

steps.title: Update Thread Preview
steps.tooltip: createMessageServer() updates last_message_at and last_message_preview on the message_threads row, and increments unread_count on all other participants' message_thread_participants rows.

steps.title: Message Appears
steps.notes: On 201: optimistic message confirmed, cache invalidated. On 422: show inline error 'Message blocked — violates community guidelines'. On 403 (block): show inline error 'You cannot send messages to this user'. useSendMessage hook handles rollback on error.

steps.title: Show Nudge Message
steps.notes: When the safety filter returns nudge_off_platform or nudge_negotiation, the API inserts a second message with type='nudge' after the user's message. The UI should render nudge messages distinctly (system message style, not attributed to the sender) to guide the user back to platform-native flows like the offer system.

paths.label: Redact
paths.detail: PII removed — filtered content stored, original in audit trail

paths.label: Nudge
paths.detail: Off-platform or negotiation attempt — message passes, nudge appended

flows.title: Archive a Thread
flows.trigger: User clicks Archive in the thread header or inbox row actions

steps.title: Archive Thread
steps.tooltip: Sets thread_status = 'archived' on the message_threads row. Archived threads are excluded from the default inbox view. Returns { success: true }. Non-participant request returns 403.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

steps.title: Thread Removed
steps.notes: On success: optimistic removal from the active inbox list, toast 'Conversation archived'. Thread should reappear if the user opens an Archived filter tab. useArchiveThread mutation should invalidate ['messaging', 'threads'].

flows.title: Make an Offer
flows.trigger: Buyer clicks Make Offer on a listing detail page

steps.title: Open Offer Form
steps.notes: Offer entry UI is Phase 3. OfferBubble component scaffold exists at src/features/messaging/components/offer-bubble/index.tsx. Should show listing price, min offer floor (70% of price), default prefill (80%), and offer amount input. Uses calculateMinOffer() and calculateDefaultOffer() from offer-validation.ts.

steps.title: Validate Offer Amount
steps.notes: Client-side pre-validation using validateOfferAmount(amountCents, listingPriceCents) before submitting. Minimum is 70% of listing price. Shows inline error if below floor.

steps.title: Submit Offer
steps.notes: Offer API routes are not yet built (Phase 3). Server service createOfferServer() is built at src/features/messaging/services/offers-server.ts. It validates: listing is active, buyer is not the seller, amount >= 70% of price, seller is valid; expires stale offers; creates inquiry thread + offer row in one operation.

steps.title: Offer Thread Opens
steps.notes: createOfferServer() creates a thread of type='offer' as part of the offer creation. The buyer should be navigated into that thread where the offer appears as an offer_node message rendered by OfferBubble.

steps.title: Seller Reviews Offer
steps.notes: Seller sees the offer_node message in the thread. OfferBubble renders offer amount, listing thumbnail, and Accept / Decline / Counter actions. acceptOfferServer(), declineOfferServer(), and counterOfferServer() are built in offers-server.ts but the API routes (/api/offers/[id]/accept etc.) are Phase 3.

steps.title: Expire Stale Offers
steps.notes: expirePendingOffersServer() is built but the cron route /api/cron/expire-offers is not yet created. Pending offers expire after 24h; accepted offers (awaiting checkout) expire after 4h. The cron should run every 15 minutes via vercel.json.

flows.title: Block a User from Thread
flows.trigger: User clicks Block in a thread header or participant context menu

steps.title: Click Block User
steps.notes: Block entry point from a messaging thread — e.g., kebab menu in the thread header with a 'Block this user' option. Client calls blockMember(memberId) from src/features/messaging/services/blocks.ts. Thread UI is Phase 3; this step is planned alongside it.

steps.title: Create Block Record
steps.tooltip: Body: { memberId }. Auth check via Supabase session. Self-block guard (400). Calls blockMemberServer() which inserts into member_blocks. Returns 201 MemberBlock. Returns 409 if already blocked.

errorCases.condition: memberId is missing from request body

steps.title: Show Block Feedback
steps.notes: On 201 or 409: show success toast (e.g., 'Blocked — You can no longer receive messages from this user.'). Thread should archive or close. The blocked user will receive 403 on any further message sends to this thread. Invalidate ['blocks', userId] so the account settings blocked-members list stays in sync. See member-block-management.json for the full block management flow.

steps.title: Show Block Error
steps.notes: On 400 or 500: show error toast (e.g., 'Could not block this user. Please try again.'). Thread remains open.

flows.title: Unread Count in Nav
flows.trigger: User loads any page — nav header fetches unread count

steps.title: Poll Unread Count
steps.tooltip: Sums unread_count across all active participant rows. Designed to be called by useUnreadCount() hook on a polling interval or query invalidation. Query key: ['messaging', 'unread'].

steps.title: Render Unread Badge
steps.notes: Nav inbox icon badge showing total unread count. Should be driven by useUnreadCount() hook. Badge should be hidden at 0 and cap display at 99+. Needs to invalidate when markThreadRead succeeds.
